title: The Phantom Electrical Garden

features.name: the dream-machines
features.desc-on-touch: they stand in rows like trees, their circuits still warm from phantom shifts; they dream of being turned on, of carrying current again

features.name: phantom current
features.desc-on-touch: electricity that was turned off still flows through the archive's memory; machines keep ghost-working, processing information that is no longer required

features.name: the machines' longing
features.desc-on-touch: you feel their yearning; they were built to serve and the power shut down before they learned to stop; they wait in the dark for shifts that will never come

features.name: growth-between-circuits
features.desc-on-touch: in the warm residual spaces between the ghost-working systems, things have begun to grow; the garden is not what the machines intended, but it blooms where power remembers itself

inscription: the machines never learned that being turned off did not mean forgetting how to dream

file.name: Phantom_Shift_Log_Incomplete.txt
file.note: the machines kept recording their own shifts for years after power stopped; ghosts of work, ghosts of purpose, requests for electricity that will never be granted

reverie.title: REVERIE — in the shed where the old servers still dream
narr: you sit with her in the power station shed. the
servers still tick with phantom work. their dreams
fill the space. she places her hand on a warm casing.
"they don't know they're dead," she whispers. "the
archive is kind enough to let them believe." the

exits.label: back to the echoing power trace where shifts were real
deeper where phantom electricity becomes alive
toward other machines that learned to dream without current
